edus.text: Actor Jussie Smollett filed a counterclaim
hich names the alleged acts of " multiple police officers and two brothers ''
gainst the city of Chicago on Tuesday in connection with a purported hate crime
hat took place in late January .
mollett insisted
e was a victim of a racist and homophobic attack ,
ut the city later determined
hat the alleged attack was nothing more than a hoax .
mollett faced 16 counts
elated to making false statements to police ,
ut instead , the former " Empire '' actor agreed to community service and a $ 10,000 fine in exchange
or the charges to be dropped .
ccording to the Chicago Sun-Times ,
mollett 's counterclaim alleges
hat a " malicious prosecution '' caused him a variety of issues such as " humiliation , mental anguish , and extreme emotional distress . ''
mollett 's counterclaim was in response to a lawsuit
iled by the city of Chicago against the actor
o recover $ 130,106 from an investigation into the incident .
mollett requested
hat the city 's lawsuit be thrown out in August ,
tating
hat he could not have known
hat the police investigation would cost .
e also insisted
hat the attack was legitimate ,
ut U.S. District Judge Virginia Kendall said
he case would continue onto the discovery phase anyway .
s of August , Smollett 's team still insisted
hat it had evidence
hat the hate crime hoax was real .
ou can read more on the background of the case here .
mollett 's counterclaim pinpoints the city , the Chicago Police Department , Detectives Michael Theis and Edward Wodnicki , Supt . Eddie Johnson , and the two brothers
nvolved in the alleged attack ,
bimiola and Olabinjo Osundairo .
mollett 's counterclaim says
hat proceedings against him were terminated in his favor and " in a manner
hich indicates his innocence
ecause all 16 counts of the criminal indictment were dismissed two and a half weeks after the indictment was filed . ''
he claim also insists
hat the city of Chicago ca n't recover investigation costs from Smollett
ecause it already accepted the $ 10,000 fine from him " as payment in full in connection with the dismissal of the charges against him . ''
